questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: According to the essay, which of the following is TRUE?
options: In the case of The Expected One, there is no telling whether it proves time travel is real.
It is definitely untrue that a Chinese archaeologist found a Swiss watch in a 400-year-old coffin.
Most of the time, we find it quite easy to determine whether a time travel story is true or not.
People have different theories about why some figures in Mayan wall paintings look so modern.
questions.explanation: 根據這篇文章,下列何者「為真」? 則情況不會奇蹟似地一夕轉好?而倘若情況沒有開始改 (A) 在《被期待的人》這幅畫的案例中,根本無法確 善,還要多久我們會成為像恐龍一般的絕跡物種?」 知是否這幅畫證實時光旅行為真有其事。

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Why did The Expected One go viral on the Internet?
options: Because it made the smartphone possible.
Because it got us fond of the smartphone.
Because it seemed related to time travel.
Because it seemed important to time travel.
questions.explanation: 《被期待的人》這幅畫為何在網路上瘋狂流傳? 此昂貴。 (A) 因為這幅畫讓智慧型手機得以發明。 (B) 整個北美大陸都遭受「炸彈氣旋」的襲擊。 (B) 因為這幅畫讓我們愛上智慧型手機。 (C) 佛羅里達這個陽光之州成為全美唯一溫暖的地方。 (C) 因為這幅畫似乎與時光旅行有關。 (D) 超過 4000 架航班因為駕駛員被凍死而取消。 (D) 因為這幅畫似乎對時光旅行相當重要。

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: What did Gerald Weinpolter tell us?
options: It’s meaningless to say that the girl in The Expected One is reading a prayer book.
By no means is the girl l in The Expected One a Christian going to church.
The girl in The Expected One is doing what most Christians of her time did.
The girl in The Expected One, as everyone knows, is indeed using a smartphone.
questions.explanation: Gerald Weinpolter 告訴我們何事? (A) 就和美國一樣,澳洲也是 「炸彈氣旋」 的受害者, (A) 說在《被期待的人》這幅畫中的女孩在閱讀一本 所以天氣才如此酷熱。 祈禱書,其實毫無意義。 (B) 在澳洲幾個主要都市內的重要幹道上行車都相當 (B) 在《被期待的人》這幅畫中的女孩,肯定不是一 危險。 位正要去教堂的基督徒。 (C) 49 起被通報的森林大火這個事件,就和 2009 黑 (C) 在《被期待的人》這幅畫中的女孩,正在做當時 色星期六一般,是個大災害。 基督徒都會做的事。 (D) 維多利亞省當地的消防局在撲滅森林大火時,共 (D) 眾所週知,在《被期待的人》這幅畫中的女孩, 有 173 人喪生。 的確是在用智慧型手機。

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: What does the author mean by “ sometimes our mind chooses to see what it wants to see, leading us to believe what we want to believe! ”?
options: Seeing is said to be believing.
Seeing is supposed to be believing.

Seeing can’t always believing. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-41 題。 Last December, the so-called “bomb cyclone,” or a terribly severe winter storm, struck the entire East Coast of the U.S. and caused horrible damage. So far it had already claimed 17 lives. Several major international airports, including JFK International Airport and Long Island MacArthur Airport, were shut down because of the heavy snow, causing over 4000 flights to be cancelled. Florida, the State of Sunshine, experienced its first snowfall in 30 years. Of course, New York suffered even more! As its local temperature dropped to  19 F , a state of emergency was declared immediately. (According to some news media, it was even warmer on Mars!) The unusually cold weather also sent the price of natural gas sky-rocketing. The New York City government estimated that it had already gone up by 600%! At the same time, however, things were completely different on the other side of the earth. Australia was having a scorching hot summer! The average temperatures in its major cities, such as Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane, were all higher than 104°F. The asphalt surface of Hume Highway near Melbourne was even melting, creating a very dangerous situation for drivers. In addition, 49 forest fires had been reported in Victoria of Southeastern Australia, which had resulted in 173 deaths so far! The local fire department called it “the biggest disaster since the Black Saturday of 2009,” stating that things had gone completely out of control because of the extremely hot and dry weather. Of course, the above is another instance of the extreme weather the whole world is currently experiencing. In a way, it is also a warning for us that our global climate system is getting worse. Indeed, we must ask ourselves these questions, “Why haven’t we realized that things won’t magically turn around unless we take immediate action? And if things don’t start getting better, how long will it be before we become an extinct species, like the dinosaurs?”
questions.explanation: 作者說: 「有時我們的內心會選擇性地去看想看的部 (A) 能被預期 (B) 能被探究 (C) 會改善 (D) 會發展 份,引導我們相信想去相信的事物。」這句話的意思

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Where do you think the essay come from?
options: a book about why tourists love America and Australia.
a website called “The News Events You Must Know.”
a report on how the global climate system was formed.
a program called “The Biggest Mysteries of Our World.”
questions.explanation: 正解：(B)；來源：106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: According to the essay, which of the following is TRUE?
options: New York was colder than Mars, so its price of natural gas became so high.
The entire North American continent was hit by the “bomb cyclone.”
Florida, the State of Sunshine, was the only warm place in the U.S.
Over 4000 flights were cancelled because their pilots had frozen to death.
questions.explanation: 正解：(A)；來源：106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: From the essay, we can know that.
options: like the U.S., Australia fell victim to the “bomb cyclone” and became extremely hot.
it was very dangerous to drive on every major highway in all of Australia’s major cities.
the incident of the 49 reported forest fires, like the 2009 Black Saturday, was a huge disaster.
173 people from the local fire department of Victoria were killed while putting out the forest fires.
questions.explanation: 正解：(C)；來源：106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: When you say, “things will turn around,” you mean things will.
questions.explanation: 正解：(C)；來源：106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: What does the author tell us in the last paragraph?
options: Everyone in the world has noticed all the major warning signs of the extreme weather.
We won’t become an extinct species if our global climate system remains the same.
We must do something quickly in order to prevent our global climate system from getting worse.

Even if we don’t take any action, our planet is still capable of fighting the extreme weather. 第二部分:非選擇題 ( 第 I 至 III 題,每題 6 分,共 18 分 ) I. 填充 說明: ▲請依據中文提示,將試題內空格○ 1 、○ 2 答案之完整單字 ( 含提示之字首 ),分別作答於答 案卷之作答欄○ 1 、○ 2 之指定範圍內以完成句子。 ▲請勿抄題,否則不予計分。每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分。
questions.explanation: 正解：(C)；來源：106-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf
